feed.title: Augustin Chan
feed.subtitle: Building systems that reason. Writing about AI, Web3, and software engineering.

entry.title: Vibe-Coded Evals with LLM-as-a-Judge
entry.summary: Using Claude Code and OpenRouter infrastructure to rapidly build model evaluations with Claude Opus 4.5 as the judge

entry.title: Why I Open-Sourced a Deterministic I-Ching Engine (Or: The Smallest Verifiable Compute You Can Build)
entry.summary: Extracting the deterministic core of 8-Bit Oracle into a standalone library, and why 'same seed, same hexagram, every time' matters more than you'd think for decentralized AI.

entry.title: Testing Twelve AI Image Models with Marcel Proust's Madeleine (Or: How We Chose Hunyuan for Hexagram 24)
entry.summary: Upgrading 8-Bit Oracle's Hexagram 24: Return with Proust's famous involuntary memory scene, then systematically testing 12 diverse AI image models through fal.ai to find which one actually captures that precise moment when past and present collapse together.

entry.title: On Going Legit on Twitter/X (Or: How I Learned to Stop Scraping and Love the Rate Limit)
entry.summary: A three-day journey through Twitter API v2, v1.1, OAuth, rate limits, and the gradual acceptance that you can't actually hack your way around free-tier constraints

entry.title: LLMs Love Markdown. CMSs Love Databases. I Built a WordPress Bridge Nobody Should Need.
entry.summary: A technical post-mortem on why WordPress's REST API is theoretically elegant but practically unusable for CI/CD, how SiteGround's security theater blocks legitimate automation, and why SSH + WP-CLI is the uncomfortable truth about 'modern' CMS workflows.

entry.title: The Daily Hexagram: A Journey from Complexity to Simplicity
entry.summary: How we learned to stop worrying and love the binary calendar. On deleting databases, removing cron jobs, and discovering that the oracle was encoded in the date all along.

entry.title: Memory Systems and the Graph That Wasn't: On Not Using Neo4j
entry.summary: What we actually ship in 8-Bit Oracle today: JSONB conversations, hexagram context, and Postgres views — plus why we didn't adopt Neo4j, MemMachine, or mem0 (yet).

entry.title: Memory Systems Deep Dive: Technical Appendix
entry.summary: Complete technical comparison of MemMachine vs mem0, PostgreSQL graph alternatives (recursive CTEs, ltree, adjacency lists), and detailed implementation strategies for AI agent memory systems.

entry.title: Tech Noir, Joy Division, and the Crushing Anxiety of Optimizing for Search Engines That Might Not Exist in Three Years
entry.summary: On replacing Wilhelm with Unknown Pleasures, cultural anchoring vs keyword stuffing, and whether we're all just shouting into the void while ChatGPT eats our lunch. Also: footnotes.

entry.title: When LLM Reasoning Becomes the Pattern - Meta-Classification Failure Modes
entry.summary: Hard constraints beat soft self-regulation when an LLM's reasoning converges to a template.

entry.title: Evolutionary ADRs: Writing Architecture Decision Records from First Principles
entry.summary: Why creating ADRs during development, not before it, leads to more honest and valuable documentation of your architectural decisions.

entry.title: Trusting Your Instincts: When AI Suggests Complex Solutions
entry.summary: A debugging session reveals why your gut feeling about 'two sets of items' was right - and how to guide AI toward simpler, better solutions.

entry.title: From Mimicry to Authenticity: Systematic Voice Evolution in DSPy
entry.summary: Replace style copying with principled search: explore a multi-dimensional voice space and use Pareto-optimal selection to shape an original AI voice.

entry.title: Locale Migration Strategy: When Adding Beats Replacing
entry.summary: A technical deep-dive into how we avoided a risky database migration by choosing an additive strategy for adding Simplified Chinese support to our Traditional Chinese implementation.

entry.title: Claude Code: From 30% to 50% Better Than Cursor Agent Mode
entry.summary: My experience with Claude Code's productivity gains, the double-edged sword of rapid development, and lessons learned from over-engineering features.

entry.title: Mem0's Aggressive `infer=True` Behavior: When LLM Filtering Goes Too Far
entry.summary: Exploring why Mem0's default LLM prompts are overly restrictive, causing the `infer=True` mode to filter out too much content. Includes root cause analysis and practical solutions for less aggressive memory management.

entry.title: Getting Browser Tools MCP Server Working with Claude Code: A Simple 2-Step Solution
entry.summary: Quick guide to configure browser-tools-mcp AgentDeskAI with Claude Code - solving connection issues and getting your MCP server properly integrated.

entry.title: DSPy + OpenRouter: Why the Standard Integration Falls Short (And How to Fix It)
entry.summary: The standard DSPy OpenRouter integration lacks failover and proper app attribution. Here's how to fix it with custom headers to get automatic model failover and your own app name on OpenRouter leaderboards.

entry.title: Integrating RevenueCat Web Billing in React/Next.js: A Practical Guide
entry.summary: A step-by-step guide to integrating RevenueCat Web Billing in a plain React/Next.js app. Covers account setup, SDK usage, code samples, and key gotchas for a smooth subscription experience.

entry.title: Cache Me If You Can: Getting Service Worker Update Toasts to Play Nice with New Jekyll Layouts
entry.summary: A dive into debugging service worker caching and update notifications for new pages in a Jekyll site, ensuring the 'Content Refresh' toast works as expected.

entry.title: Exploring 'Futures Thinking': A Design Dive for 8-Bit Oracle
entry.summary: A technical exploration of 'Futures Thinking,' a potential experimental feature for 8-Bit Oracle, looking at a Triple Hex Mode and AI-driven scenario mapping.

entry.title: Android Passkeys: Frontend Adventures with CredentialManager and Jetpack Compose
entry.summary: Building the Android frontend for FIDO2 passkeys: tackling CredentialManager, migrating DataStore, and the joys of debugging in Jetpack Compose.

entry.title: FIDO2 Passkeys for Android: Backend Challenges in Python
entry.summary: A developer's journey through the challenges of implementing FIDO2 passkeys for an Android app using Python, covering assetlinks.json, data serialization, and Base64URL encoding.

entry.title: Deploying a CosmWasm Smart Contract on the Stargaze Testnet
entry.summary: A developer's guide to deploying a CosmWasm smart contract on the Stargaze testnet, covering environment setup, starsd CLI usage, contract uploading, instantiation, and Rust version considerations.

entry.title: Revolutionizing Verifiable Compute on Solana: Key Bonsol Enhancements
entry.summary: Deep dive into recent Bonsol framework improvements: a new I Ching sample dApp, RISC0_DEV_MODE integration for faster development, and custom S3 URL support for artifact flexibility.

entry.title: DKG OriginTrail Integration - Consensus HK 2025 Hackathon Win
entry.summary: Integrated OriginTrail DKG with Eliza AI and a custom divination backend, enabling on-chain persistence of divinations via a Telegram bot.

entry.title: Brainstorming a New Theory of Self-Directed Learning for AI, with AI
entry.summary: An exploration of using the I-Ching's King Wen sequence as a potential framework for self-directed learning in AGI. Details the thought process, key insights, and implications for AI development, including a link to a research paper.

entry.title: Key Contributions to ai16z Eliza AI Project (Nov 2024)
entry.summary: Detailing significant enhancements to the ai16z Eliza AI project, focusing on local-first capabilities, model optimization, and backend stability.

entry.title: Zustand: A Weapon for Efficient User State Management in React
entry.summary: An overview of Zustand as an efficient state management library for React, highlighting its benefits over traditional methods (refs, Context API) for managing user state, simplifying code, and minimizing re-renders.

entry.title: Optimizing React Authentication: Minimizing Re-renders and Improving Performance
entry.summary: A guide to minimizing re-renders and optimizing performance in React/Next.js applications with authentication. Covers combining state, memoizing context with useMemo, tracking state changes with useRef, debouncing, and optimizing useEffect dependencies.

entry.title: Conditional Rendering in Next.js: Hiding Footer on Specific Pages
entry.summary: A guide to conditionally rendering components, like a footer, in a Next.js 14 (App Router) application by bridging server and client components using React Context API and usePathname.

entry.title: Integrating with Shadcn: Tips and Insights for Seamless UI Component Integration
entry.summary: A developer's experience integrating Shadcn UI components into a Next.js project. Covers project preparation, handling opinionated styling (dark theme, New York style), managing global CSS, and an example root layout incorporating ThemeProvider and Toaster.

entry.title: Securing SSH: A Detailed Look at Fail2Ban Configuration and Rule Management
entry.summary: A detailed guide to troubleshooting and configuring Fail2Ban with UFW for robust SSH security. Covers iptables rule order, jail.local setup, persistent bans, and ensuring proper cleanup of rules on service restart.

entry.title: Navigating UI Framework Transitions: From Bootstrap to Shadcn
entry.summary: A developer's journey through UI framework choices, from Bootstrap to Next.js with Shadcn/ui. Discusses the pros and cons of component libraries like Shadcn (built on Radix UI and Tailwind CSS) versus traditional UI frameworks, and touches on project MVP plans.

entry.title: Navigating Client vs Server-Side Authentication in Web Development
entry.summary: Explores client-side vs. server-side authentication using Supabase with Next.js 14 (App Router). Covers Google OAuth2 (client-side flow with createBrowserClient) and Magic Link (server-side flow with createServerClient), and clarifies Supabase Auth-UI usage.

entry.title: Implementing Auth and Database for 8-Bit Oracle
entry.summary: Details on setting up authentication and database for the 8-Bit Oracle project using Next.js, Supabase (with Auth-UI for OAuth2), and Tailwind CSS. Discusses choices like Supabase over Firebase and future consideration of Web3Auth.

entry.title: Claude vs GPT4 for Coding
entry.summary: A developer's comparison of using Claude Opus and GPT-4 (via Cursor.sh) as AI coding assistants, particularly when working with Next.js App Router vs. Pages Router and handling framework updates.

entry.title: How to Create an Interactive Typing Effect in React with Skip Animation Feature
entry.summary: A developer's notes and tips on implementing a smooth, multi-paragraph typing effect in React with a skip animation feature. Covers state management, DOM manipulation alternatives, event handling, and internationalization with react-i18next.

entry.title: Exploring Divination Techniques: Chainlink VRF vs. Traditional Randomness Methods
entry.summary: An analysis of using Chainlink VRF for I-Ching divination, comparing it to traditional methods and other randomness generation techniques like PRNGs and client-side randomness. Discusses pros, cons, and includes JavaScript examples for hexagram generation with changing lines.

entry.title: Solving Webpack Issues: Eliminating Double Script Loading and FOUC
entry.summary: Troubleshooting common Webpack issues, including double script loading due to manual and Webpack config imports, and resolving Flash of Unstyled Content (FOUC) by correctly setting production mode with cross-env and configuring MiniCssExtractPlugin.

entry.title: React's useEffect Firing Twice in Dev? The HMR Culprit and a Simple Check
entry.summary: A quick developer tip: If your React useEffect hook seems to run twice in Next.js development mode, Hot Module Reloading (HMR) might be the reason. Here's why and how to verify.

entry.title: Fortune-Telling NFTs: Weaving I-Ching Wisdom with Blockchain Technology
entry.summary: A project idea to create Fortune-Telling NFTs by integrating the ancient wisdom of the I-Ching (Yin/Yang, Trigrams, Hexagrams) with modern blockchain technology. I explore the cultural and personal motivations, the envisioned fortune-telling process, and potential Web3 integrations.

entry.title: Brainstorming an AI-Powered Photo Critic Service
entry.summary: My initial thoughts on designing an AI service that can analyze and critique uploaded photos. I explore a multi-stage approach using CNNs, CLIP, and LLMs for metadata extraction, image understanding, and generating constructive feedback.

entry.title: Cracking AWS Network Issues: EC2 Docker to RDS Postgres Connectivity
entry.summary: A step-by-step guide to diagnosing and fixing network connection problems between a Dockerized Spring Boot app on AWS EC2 and an RDS Postgres database. Learn to use nslookup, nc, psql for troubleshooting, and how to configure Docker DNS for private AWS VPC name resolution.

entry.title: Rapid Spring Boot: Notes on JWT Authentication and JPA Data Persistence
entry.summary: My developer notes on quickly getting to grips with JWT authentication and data persistence in Spring Boot 3. I cover my experiences with Spring Security, the power of JPA annotations for entity management, stateless sessions with JWT, and BCrypt password encryption, contrasting it with older Java EE approaches.

entry.title: AI for Family Stories: Transcribing, Mapping, and Visualizing Our Histories
entry.summary: Some brainstorming on using AI to transcribe family stories shared at gatherings. I'm thinking about detecting narrators, mapping complex family relationships, and even visualizing our family history as an interactive graph.

entry.title: Building a Smarter AI Agent with Haystack 1.x: My Journey with RAG and Custom Pipelines
entry.summary: My experience implementing Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) and AI Agents with Haystack 1.x. I cover conversational memory, creating custom data pipelines using FAISS DocumentStore, integrating Google Search, and the challenges of incrementally indexing new knowledge.

entry.title: Exploring Haystack: Building Advanced NLP Applications with LLMs and Vector Search
entry.summary: My practical tips and experiences from working with the Haystack LLM framework. I cover version differences (1.x vs. 2.x beta), the benefits of developing with a forked repository, managing Python dependencies for projects like this, and how to contribute back to open-source.

entry.title: Speed Up GPT-J-6B: From Minutes to Seconds with GGML Conversion
entry.summary: A guide on dramatically improving GPT-J-6B inference speed. I'll show you how I converted a Hugging Face model from its standard format to GGML, slashing wait times from minutes to seconds on my own machine. Covers the conversion process, memory needs, and running the optimized model.

entry.title: The Challenge of Finding Uncensored AI Models
entry.summary: Exploring the difficulties in finding truly uncensored AI models. I discuss the influence of OpenAI's data and look at methods for fine-tuning base models to remove built-in limitations or refusals, mentioning my experiences with some specific models.

entry.title: AI Model Formats Explained: Demystifying Llama.cpp, GGUF, GGML, and Transformers
entry.summary: A breakdown of a helpful Reddit discussion on AI model formats like GGUF, GGML, and tools such as Llama.cpp and Hugging Face Transformers. I add my own notes on GGML quantization and summarize the key takeaways for navigating this complex landscape.
